narrative.descriptive: Current AQI is 120 (Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups) based on US EPA measurement standards.
narrative.diagnostic: PM2.5 is the dominant pollutant, critically elevated at 43.3 µg/m³ — more than 3.6× the safe-exposure threshold. Pollution signature shows: vehicular emissions (NO₂: 51.4 µg/m³, CO: 994.0 µg/m³).
narrative.predictive: Forecast model projects PM2.5 largely stable (-2.9%) over the next 48 periods, moving from 60.4 to 58.6 µg/m³. Continued elevated readings are expected — plan outdoor activities accordingly.
narrative.prescriptive: Unhealthy for sensitive groups — those with asthma, cardiovascular conditions, children, and the elderly should avoid strenuous outdoor activity. Sensitive individuals should limit outdoor time, especially during peak traffic hours.
narrative.context: Dense traffic corridors along NH-44 and ring roads, heavy brick kiln activity in peripheral zones, and seasonal crop burning from neighboring states (Punjab, Haryana) are primary PM2.5 drivers. Low-wind winter inversions trap pollutants near the surface.

risk.weather: Moderate wind and temperature inversions are amplifying local accumulation.
risk.formula: ERS = 35% AQI + 20% PM2.5 + 10% PM10 + 10% NO₂ + 5% O₃ + 5% Weather + 10% Forecast + Location Factors (Industrial/Traffic/Water)
